User Story: As a development team, we want a documented API contract between the VS Code extension and the FastAPI server so that backend and frontend can develop independently without waiting on each other.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Document all endpoints (/health /scan /fix /progress /revert) with request+response schemas; define SSE event schema; example JSON for every endpoint; all three team members sign off

User Story: As a frontend developer, I want a running FastAPI server with stub endpoints returning hardcoded responses so that I can integrate the extension against real HTTP immediately without waiting for real pipeline logic.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): server/app.py starts on port 7432; /health returns ok; all endpoints return stub data; requests logged to console; requirements.txt updated with fastapi+uvicorn+sse-starlette

User Story: As a developer, I want a barebones VS Code extension that spawns the Python server on activation and proves the HTTP connection works so that we have the full communication loop verified before building real features.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): extension/ directory with valid package.json; spawns python server/app.py on activate; kills server on deactivate; calls /health and logs to Output Channel; friendly error if Python not found

User Story: As the VS Code extension, I want to POST a workspace path to /scan and receive a ranked list of real code smells detected by ReactSniffer so that the sidebar can display actionable issues.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Runs ReactSniffer subprocess on workspace path; returns smells sorted by severity; correct response shape with smell_id/smell_type/component_name/file_path; handles invalid path gracefully; responds within 60s

User Story: As a developer, I want to see all detected code smells grouped by type in a VS Code sidebar panel so that I can understand the full scope of issues in my project at a glance.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Sidebar in Activity Bar with Scan button; tree: Smell Type → File → Component; severity badges; loading spinner during scan; empty state message; error state message; works against stub data

User Story: As a developer, I want to check and uncheck individual smells (and select all by type) so that I have precise control over which code smells get fixed before I commit to running the pipeline.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Checkbox per smell leaf node; group header toggles all children; status bar shows selected count; Fix Selected button disabled when 0 selected; selection resets on new scan

User Story: As a developer, I want to see an estimated processing time and API cost before I start a fix run so that I can make an informed decision about how many smells to fix at once.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Shows est. time (count×25s÷3) and cost (count×$0.005) in sidebar; updates live on check/uncheck; confirmation dialog before starting fix with time+cost summary

User Story: As the extension, I want to POST a list of smells to /fix and have the server run the LangGraph pipeline with a concurrency limit of 3 so that fixes complete in reasonable time without overloading OpenAI rate limits or causing file write conflicts.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Non-blocking POST returns job_id immediately; max 3 concurrent workers via asyncio+ThreadPoolExecutor; per-file lock prevents concurrent edits to same file; calls runner.run_task(); GET /jobs/{job_id} returns state; tested on 5 real smells

User Story: As the VS Code extension, I want to receive live Server-Sent Events as each pipeline node completes so that the UI can show real-time progress without polling.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): GET /progress/{job_id} streams SSE; node_done events per task; task_done event with status/tactic/retry_count; run_complete event with summary; stream closes cleanly; reconnect delivers run_complete if already finished

User Story: As a developer, I want to see live progress in the sidebar and Output Channel while the pipeline runs so that I know it's working and can monitor which smells are being processed.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Spinner per smell while processing; icons update on task_done (✓/✗/↷/⚠); status bar shows count; Output Channel logs node events; Cancel button triggers DELETE /jobs; non-blocking SSE consumer; works against stub SSE

User Story: As a developer, I want to cancel a running fix job so that I can stop the pipeline mid-run if I change my mind without corrupting files already being edited.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): DELETE /jobs/{job_id} sets cancellation flag; running task completes before stop; SSE emits cancelled event; files already edited remain; GET /jobs reflects cancelled status; 404 if job not found; 409 if already complete

User Story: As a developer, I want to see a clear summary when a fix run completes so that I immediately understand how many smells were fixed, rejected, or skipped without reading the full log.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Notification on run_complete with accepted/rejected/skipped/failed counts; View Report opens Webview with results table; Revert All button in Webview; sidebar icons updated to final status

User Story: As a developer, I want to click an accepted smell in the sidebar and immediately see a side-by-side diff of what the pipeline changed so that I can review the refactor before deciding whether to keep it.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Click accepted smell opens vscode.diff; left=git original via /original endpoint; right=current file; correct diff editor title; graceful fallback if file not in git; rejected/skipped shows message instead

User Story: As the VS Code extension, I want to call a revert endpoint for a specific file so that the user can undo a pipeline edit without manually running git commands.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): POST /revert runs git checkout -- file; returns success/failure with clear error messages; GET /original returns git HEAD content; works on Windows and macOS; tested manually end-to-end

User Story: As a developer, I want a revert button on each accepted smell in the sidebar so that I can undo individual fixes I don't agree with in one click.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Revert inline action on hover for accepted smells; calls POST /revert; icon updates to reverted on success; error notification on failure; Revert All in summary Webview; already-reverted smells cannot be reverted again

User Story: As a developer installing the extension for the first time, I want it to automatically find my Python installation so that I don't need to configure anything manually before using it.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Checks ms-python extension API then python3/python on PATH; logs detected path+version to Output Channel; error if Python < 3.8; actionable error with interpreter picker button if not found; works Windows+macOS+Linux

User Story: As a developer installing the extension for the first time, I want Python dependencies installed automatically on first activation so that I don't need to manually run pip install before using the extension.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): Runs pip install -r requirements.txt on first activation; progress in Output Channel; status bar shows Setting up...; writes .deps_installed marker on success; error notification with manual command on failure; skips on subsequent activations

User Story: As a team member, I want to install the extension from a .vsix file so that I can share and test the full extension without publishing to the VS Code Marketplace.
Acceptance Criteria (Summary): vsce package produces react-refactor-0.1.0.vsix; installs cleanly via Install from VSIX; server/ vendor/ requirements.txt bundled; .vscodeignore excludes runs/ data/ experiments/ __pycache__; activates correctly post-install; README covers prerequisites and usage
